message.content:
Update the JJD-GallopsData.adoc specification to add pace context to the jjx_chalk operation.

**File to edit**: `Tools/jjk/JJD-GallopsData.adoc`

**Changes needed**:

1. In the Arguments section (around line 80-100), add a new argument definition for `--pace`:
```
[[jjda_pace]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axa_cli_option‚üß
{jjda_pace}::
Pace silks for context in commit messages.
Long: `--pace`.
Short: `-p`.
```

2. Add the attribute reference in the mapping section (around line 80):
```
:jjda_pace:           <<jjda_pace,--pace>>
```

3. Update the `jjdo_chalk` operation section (around line 1543-1573):
   - Add `--pace` to the Arguments list
   - Make it: required for APPROACH/WRAP/FLY markers, optional for DISCUSSION
   - Update the commit message format from:
     `[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX] MARKER: description`
     to:
     `[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX/pace-silks] MARKER: description`
   - Update the Behavior section to reflect the new argument

4. Also update the "Commit Message Patterns" section (around line 1431-1457) to show the new chalk format with pace context.

**Style notes**:
- Follow existing AsciiDoc patterns in the file
- Use the linked term pattern: `{jjda_pace}` references the definition
- Match the annotation style: `// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß` or `// ‚ü¶axd_conditional‚üß`

Please make these edits directly to the file.

    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí// Operations
    90‚Üí:jjdo_canter:         <<jjdo_canter,jjx_canter>>
    91‚Üí:jjdo_chalk:          <<jjdo_chalk,jjx_chalk>>
    92‚Üí:jjdo_muster:         <<jjdo_muster,jjx_muster>>
    93‚Üí:jjdo_nominate:       <<jjdo_nominate,jjx_nominate>>
    94‚Üí:jjdo_notch:          <<jjdo_notch,jjx_notch>>
    95‚Üí:jjdo_rail:           <<jjdo_rail,jjx_rail>>
    96‚Üí:jjdo_rein:           <<jjdo_rein,jjx_rein>>
    97‚Üí:jjdo_retire:         <<jjdo_retire,jjx_retire>>
    98‚Üí:jjdo_saddle:         <<jjdo_saddle,jjx_saddle>>
    99‚Üí:jjdo_slate:          <<jjdo_slate,jjx_slate>>
   100‚Üí:jjdo_tally:          <<jjdo_tally,jjx_tally>>
   101‚Üí:jjdo_validate:       <<jjdo_validate,jjx_validate>>
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí// Section headers
   104‚Üí:jjds_arguments:        <<jjds_arguments,Arguments:>>
   105‚Üí:jjds_behavior:         <<jjds_behavior,Behavior:>>
   106‚Üí:jjds_exit_enumerated:  <<jjds_exit_enumerated,Exit Status:>>
   107‚Üí:jjds_exit_uniform:     <<jjds_exit_uniform,Exit Status:>>
   108‚Üí:jjds_stdout:           <<jjds_stdout,Stdout:>>
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí// Serialization
   111‚Üí:jjdz_charset:  <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
   112‚Üí:jjdz_encoding: <<jjdz_encoding,Identity Encoding>>
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí// end::mapping-section[]
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí== Overview
   117‚Üí
   118‚ÜíThe
   119‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   120‚Üíis the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   121‚ÜíIt tracks
   122‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   123‚Üí(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   124‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   125‚Üí(discrete actions).
   126‚ÜíEach
   127‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   128‚Üímaintains a history of
   129‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   130‚Üí(plan refinements).
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí== Design Principles
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   135‚Üí
   136‚ÜíAll access to the
   137‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   138‚Üígoes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   139‚ÜíNo jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   140‚ÜíThis is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   141‚Üí
   142‚ÜíThe rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   143‚ÜíThe internet is full of bad bash.
   144‚ÜíRust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   145‚ÜíThe compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí=== Git as Journal
   148‚Üí
   149‚ÜíThe
   150‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   151‚Üíremains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   152‚ÜíEvery mutation creates a diffable commit.
   153‚ÜíGit owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   154‚Üí
   155‚ÜíThe
   156‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   157‚Üíhistory within each
   158‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   159‚Üíexists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   160‚ÜíGit has the full history, but
   161‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   162‚Üílet us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   165‚Üí
   166‚ÜíNew
   167‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   168‚Üíprepend to position 0 rather than append.
   169‚ÜíThis produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   170‚ÜíSmall discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   171‚Üí
   172‚ÜíThe
   173‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   174‚Üíarray contains full
   175‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   176‚ÜíEach
   177‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   178‚Üíembeds its parent
   179‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   180‚Üí(redundant within a single
   181‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}).
   182‚ÜíThis redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   185‚Üí
   186‚ÜíBash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   187‚Üí{jjdx_cli} owns all
   188‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   189‚Üífile access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   190‚Üí{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   191‚ÜíIf {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   192‚Üí
   193‚ÜíThe pattern:
   194‚Üí[source,bash]
   195‚Üí----
   196‚Üílock_acquire
   197‚Üívvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   198‚Üílock_release
   199‚Üí----
   200‚Üí
   201‚ÜíThis keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí=== Identity Encoding
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   206‚Üí(`‚Ç£` prefix) and
   207‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   208‚Üí(`‚Ç¢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   209‚ÜíThe base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   210‚ÜíInternally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   211‚Üí
   212‚Üí=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   213‚Üí
   214‚ÜíJob Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   215‚ÜíThis is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   218‚Üí* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   219‚Üí* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   220‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `‚Ç£`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   221‚Üí* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `‚Ç¢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   222‚Üí* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   223‚Üí* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   224‚Üí
   225‚Üí== Types
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üí[[jjdt_firemark]]
   228‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   229‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}::
   230‚ÜíThe identity of a
   231‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
   232‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç£AB`).
   233‚ÜíThe 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   234‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   235‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç£` prefix; output always includes it.
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí[[jjdt_coronet]]
   238‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   239‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}::
   240‚ÜíThe identity of a
   241‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}.
   242‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç¢ABCDE`).
   243‚ÜíGlobally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   244‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   245‚Üíencoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   246‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   247‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç¢` prefix; output always includes it.
   248‚Üí
   249‚Üí[[jjdt_silks]]
   250‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   251‚Üí{jjdt_silks}::
   252‚ÜíA kebab-case display name.
   253‚ÜíUsed for human-readable identification of
   254‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   255‚Üíand
   256‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   257‚ÜíPattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí== Records
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   262‚Üí
   263‚Üí[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   264‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   265‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}::
   266‚ÜíThe root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   267‚ÜíSingleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   268‚Üí
   269‚Üí==== Members
   270‚Üí
   271‚Üí* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   272‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   273‚Üí{jjdgm_seed}
   274‚Üíis the next
   275‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   276‚Üíto allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   277‚ÜíIncremented by
   278‚Üí{jjdo_nominate}.
   279‚Üí
   280‚Üí=== {jjdhr_heat}
   281‚Üí
   282‚Üí[[jjdhr_heat]]
   283‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   284‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}::
   285‚ÜíA bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   286‚ÜíContains zero or more
   287‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   288‚Üírepresenting discrete actions.
   289‚ÜíKeyed in JSON by
   290‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   291‚Üí
   292‚Üí==== Members
   293‚Üí
   294‚Üí* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   295‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   296‚Üí{jjdhm_firemark}
   297‚Üíis the
   298‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   299‚Üíprimary key.
   300‚ÜíType:
   301‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   302‚ÜíImplicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   303‚Üí
   304‚Üí* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   305‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   306‚Üí{jjdhm_silks}
   307‚Üíis the
   308‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   309‚Üídisplay name.
   310‚ÜíType:
   311‚Üí{jjdt_silks}.
   312‚Üí
   313‚Üí* [[jjdhm_created]]
   314‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   315‚Üí{jjdhm_created}
   316‚Üírecords when the
   317‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   318‚Üíwas nominated.
   319‚ÜíFormat: YYMMDD.
   320‚Üí
   321‚Üí* [[jjdhm_status]]
   322‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   323‚Üí{jjdhm_status}
   324‚Üíindicates the
   325‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   326‚Üílifecycle state.
   327‚ÜíValues:
   328‚Üí{jjdhe_current}
   329‚Üíor
   330‚Üí{jjdhe_retired}.
   331‚Üí
   332‚Üí* [[jjdhm_order]]
   333‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated‚üß
   334‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   335‚Üílists
   336‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   337‚Üíin execution sequence.
   338‚ÜíMay be reordered via
   339‚Üí{jjdo_rail}.
   340‚Üí
   341‚Üí* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   342‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   343‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}
   344‚Üíis the next
   345‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   346‚Üíindex to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   347‚ÜíIncremented by
   348‚Üí{jjdo_slate}.
   349‚Üí
   350‚Üí* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   351‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   352‚Üí{jjdhm_paddock}
   353‚Üíis the path to the paddock file containing
   354‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   355‚Üícontext and background.
   356‚ÜíFormat: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   357‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   358‚Üí(without `‚Ç£` prefix).
   359‚ÜíCreated by
   360‚Üí{jjdo_nominate}.
   361‚Üí
   362‚Üí==== Status Values
   363‚Üí
   364‚Üí[[jjdhe_current]]
   365‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   366‚Üí{jjdhe_current}::
   367‚ÜíThe
   368‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   369‚Üíis active and accepting work.
   370‚Üí
   371‚Üí[[jjdhe_retired]]
   372‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   373‚Üí{jjdhe_retired}::
   374‚ÜíThe
   375‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   376‚Üíis complete and archived.
   377‚Üí
   378‚Üí=== {jjdpr_pace}
   379‚Üí
   380‚Üí[[jjdpr_pace]]
   381‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   382‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}::
   383‚ÜíA discrete action within a
   384‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
   385‚ÜíContains one or more
   386‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   387‚Üírepresenting state snapshots and plan refinements.
   388‚ÜíCurrent state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   389‚ÜíKeyed in JSON by
   390‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}.
   391‚Üí
   392‚Üí==== Members
   393‚Üí
   394‚Üí* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   395‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   396‚Üí{jjdpm_coronet}
   397‚Üíis the
   398‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_p}
   399‚Üíglobally unique identity.
   400‚ÜíType:
   401‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}.
   402‚ÜíImplicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   403‚Üí
   404‚Üí* [[jjdpm_silks]]
   405‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   406‚Üí{jjdpm_silks}
   407‚Üíis the
   408‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_p}
   409‚Üídisplay name.
   410‚ÜíType:
   411‚Üí{jjdt_silks}.
   412‚Üí
   413‚Üí=== {jjdkr_tack}
   414‚Üí
   415‚Üí[[jjdkr_tack]]
   416‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   417‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}::
   418‚ÜíA snapshot of
   419‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   420‚Üístate and plan at a point in time.
   421‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   422‚Üíform an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   423‚ÜíPosition 0 is the most recent; older
   424‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   425‚Üífollow.
   426‚ÜíNew
   427‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   428‚Üíare prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   429‚ÜíThe current
   430‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   431‚Üístate is always
   432‚Üí`tacks[0].state`.
   433‚Üí
   434‚Üí==== Members
   435‚Üí
   436‚Üí* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   437‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   438‚Üí{jjdkm_refined}
   439‚Üírecords when this
   440‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   441‚Üíwas added.
   442‚ÜíFormat: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   443‚Üí
   444‚Üí* [[jjdkm_state]]
   445‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   446‚Üí{jjdkm_state}
   447‚Üíindicates the
   448‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_p}
   449‚Üíprogress at this snapshot.
   450‚ÜíValues:
   451‚Üí{jjdpe_rough},
   452‚Üí{jjdpe_primed},
   453‚Üí{jjdpe_complete},
   454‚Üíor
   455‚Üí{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   456‚Üí
   457‚Üí* [[jjdkm_text]]
   458‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   459‚Üí{jjdkm_text}
   460‚Üícontains the plan specification.
   461‚ÜíNon-empty string describing what the
   462‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   463‚Üíshould accomplish.
   464‚Üí
   465‚Üí* [[jjdkm_direction]]
   466‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional‚üß
   467‚Üí{jjdkm_direction}
   468‚Üícontains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   469‚ÜíRequired when
   470‚Üí{jjdkm_state}
   471‚Üíis
   472‚Üí{jjdpe_primed};
   473‚Üíforbidden otherwise.
   474‚ÜíDescribes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.
   475‚Üí
   476‚Üí==== State Values
   477‚Üí
   478‚Üí[[jjdpe_rough]]
   479‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   480‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}::
   481‚ÜíThe
   482‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   483‚Üíneeds clarification before work can proceed.
   484‚ÜíWhen context is assembled for a
   485‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}
   486‚Üípace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.
   487‚Üí
   488‚Üí[[jjdpe_primed]]
   489‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   490‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}::
   491‚ÜíThe
   492‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   493‚Üíis ready for autonomous execution.
   494‚ÜíThe
   495‚Üí{jjdkm_direction}
   496‚Üífield contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
   497‚ÜíWhen context is assembled for a
   498‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}
   499‚Üípace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.
   500‚Üí
   501‚Üí[[jjdpe_complete]]
   502‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   503‚Üí{jjdpe_complete}::
   504‚ÜíThe
   505‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   506‚Üíhas been successfully finished.
   507‚Üí
   508‚Üí[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
   509‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   510‚Üí{jjdpe_abandoned}::
   511‚ÜíThe
   512‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   513‚Üíwas stopped without completion.
   514‚Üí
   515‚Üí== Serialization
   516‚Üí
   517‚Üí[[jjdz_charset]]
   518‚Üí{jjdz_charset}::
   519‚ÜíThe 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   520‚ÜíUses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 ¬ß5):
   521‚Üí`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   522‚ÜíPosition in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   523‚ÜíThis alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   524‚ÜíMatches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   525‚Üí
   526‚Üí[[jjdz_encoding]]
   527‚Üí{jjdz_encoding}::
   528‚ÜíThe algorithm for encoding
   529‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   530‚Üíand
   531‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   532‚Üívalues.
   533‚Üí+
   534‚Üí*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   535‚Üí+
   536‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters
   537‚Üí. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `‚Ç£` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   538‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç£XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   539‚Üí+
   540‚Üí*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   541‚Üí+
   542‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters
   543‚Üí. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   544‚Üí. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   545‚Üí. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `‚Ç¢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   546‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç¢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   547‚Üí+
   548‚Üí*Input flexibility:*
   549‚Üí+
   550‚ÜíCLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   551‚ÜíLength determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   552‚ÜíOutput always includes the prefix (`‚Ç£` or `‚Ç¢`).
   553‚Üí

   645‚Üí== CLI
   646‚Üí
   647‚Üí[[jjdx_vvx]]
   648‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_program‚üß
   649‚Üí{jjdx_vvx}::
   650‚ÜíThe Voce Viva executor.
   651‚ÜíLocal reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.
   652‚Üí
   653‚Üí[[jjdx_cli]]
   654‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_command_group‚üß
   655‚Üí{jjdx_cli}::
   656‚ÜíThe Job Jockey command group within
   657‚Üí{jjdx_vvx}.
   658‚ÜíInvoked as `vvx jjx`.
   659‚ÜíAll operations are subcommands of
   660‚Üí{jjdx_cli}.
   661‚ÜíInherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
   662‚Üí
   663‚Üí== Operations
   664‚Üí
   665‚ÜíOperations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
   666‚Üí{jjdx_cli} owns all
   667‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   668‚Üífile access (read, transform, atomic write).
   669‚ÜíBash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
   670‚Üí{jjda_file}.
   671‚Üí
   672‚Üí=== Write Operations
   673‚Üí
   674‚Üí[[jjdo_nominate]]
   675‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
   676‚Üí==== {jjdo_nominate}
   677‚Üí
   678‚ÜíCreate a new
   679‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   680‚Üíwith empty
   681‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   682‚Üístructure.
   683‚Üí
   684‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
   685‚Üí
   686‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
   687‚Üí* {jjda_file}
   688‚Üí
   689‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   690‚Üí* {jjda_silks}
   691‚Üí
   692‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   693‚Üí* {jjda_created}
   694‚Üí
   695‚Üí{jjds_stdout} New
   696‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   697‚Üí
   698‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   699‚Üí
   700‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   701‚Üí
   702‚Üí. Read
   703‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   704‚Üífrom file
   705‚Üí. Read current
   706‚Üí{jjdgm_seed}
   707‚Üías
   708‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   709‚Üí. Compute paddock path: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the 2 base64 chars
   710‚Üí. Create paddock file with template:
   711‚Üí+
   712‚Üí----
   713‚Üí# Paddock: {silks}
   714‚Üí
   715‚Üí## Context
   716‚Üí
   717‚Üí(Describe the initiative's background and goals)
   718‚Üí
   719‚Üí## References
   720‚Üí
   721‚Üí(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)
   722‚Üí----
   723‚Üí. Create new
   724‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   725‚Üíwith:
   726‚Üí.. Key: `‚Ç£` + encoded
   727‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   728‚Üí.. {jjdhm_silks}:
   729‚Üífrom argument
   730‚Üí.. {jjdhm_created}:
   731‚Üífrom argument
   732‚Üí.. {jjdhm_status}:
   733‚Üí{jjdhe_current}
   734‚Üí.. {jjdhm_order}:
   735‚Üíempty array
   736‚Üí.. {jjdhm_seed}:
   737‚Üí`AAA` (index 0)
   738‚Üí.. {jjdhm_paddock}:
   739‚Üícomputed paddock path
   740‚Üí.. `paces`: empty object
   741‚Üí. Increment
   742‚Üí{jjdgm_seed}
   743‚Üí(base64 arithmetic with carry)
   744‚Üí. Write transformed
   745‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   746‚Üíto file (atomic)
   747‚Üí. Output new
   748‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   749‚Üíto stdout
   750‚Üí
   751‚Üí[[jjdo_slate]]
   752‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
   753‚Üí==== {jjdo_slate}
   754‚Üí
   755‚ÜíAdd a new
   756‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   757‚Üíto a
   758‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
   759‚ÜíThe initial
   760‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   761‚Üíis created with state
   762‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}.
   763‚Üí
   764‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
   765‚Üí
   766‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
   767‚Üí* {jjda_file}
   768‚Üí
   769‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   770‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
   771‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî target
   772‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   773‚Üí
   774‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   775‚Üí* {jjda_silks}
   776‚Üí
   777‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   778‚Üí* stdin ‚Äî
   779‚Üí{jjdkm_text}
   780‚Üícontent for the initial
   781‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   782‚Üí
   783‚Üí{jjds_stdout} New
   784‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   785‚Üí
   786‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   787‚Üí
   788‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   789‚Üí
   790‚Üí. Read
   791‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   792‚Üífrom file
   793‚Üí. Verify
   794‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   795‚Üíexists with given
   796‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   797‚Üí. Read
   798‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}
   799‚Üífrom target
   800‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   801‚Üí. Construct
   802‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}:
   803‚Üí`‚Ç¢` + encoded
   804‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   805‚Üí(2 chars) +
   806‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}
   807‚Üí(3 chars)
   808‚Üí. Create new
   809‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   810‚Üíwith:
   811‚Üí.. Key: constructed
   812‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   813‚Üí.. {jjdpm_silks}:
   814‚Üífrom argument
   815‚Üí.. `tacks`: array with single
   816‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}:
   817‚Üí... {jjdkm_refined}:
   818‚Üícurrent timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
   819‚Üí... {jjdkm_state}:
   820‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}
   821‚Üí... {jjdkm_text}:
   822‚Üífrom stdin
   823‚Üí. Append
   824‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   825‚Üíto
   826‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   827‚Üí. Increment
   828‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}
   829‚Üí(base64 arithmetic with carry)
   830‚Üí. Write transformed
   831‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   832‚Üíto file (atomic)
   833‚Üí. Output new
   834‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   835‚Üíto stdout
   836‚Üí
   837‚Üí[[jjdo_rail]]
   838‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
   839‚Üí==== {jjdo_rail}
   840‚Üí
   841‚ÜíReorder
   842‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   843‚Üíwithin a
   844‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   845‚Üíby updating
   846‚Üí{jjdhm_order}.
   847‚Üí
   848‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
   849‚Üí
   850‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
   851‚Üí* {jjda_file}
   852‚Üí
   853‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   854‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
   855‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî target
   856‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   857‚Üí
   858‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   859‚Üí* order ‚Äî array of
   860‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   861‚Üíin desired sequence (JSON array or space-separated)
   862‚Üí
   863‚Üí{jjds_stdout} None
   864‚Üí
   865‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   866‚Üí
   867‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   868‚Üí
   869‚Üí. Read
   870‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   871‚Üífrom file
   872‚Üí. Verify
   873‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   874‚Üíexists with given
   875‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   876‚Üí. Parse order argument into array of
   877‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   878‚Üí. Validate order array:
   879‚Üí.. Count must match current
   880‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   881‚Üílength
   882‚Üí.. No duplicate
   883‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   884‚Üí.. All
   885‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   886‚Üímust exist in
   887‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   888‚Üí`paces` object
   889‚Üí.. All
   890‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   891‚Üímust embed correct parent
   892‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   893‚Üí. Replace
   894‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   895‚Üíwith new order array
   896‚Üí. Write transformed
   897‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   898‚Üíto file (atomic)
   899‚Üí
   900‚Üí[[jjdo_tally]]
   901‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
   902‚Üí==== {jjdo_tally}
   903‚Üí
   904‚ÜíAdd a new
   905‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   906‚Üíto a
   907‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}.
   908‚ÜíThis is the unified operation for all
   909‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   910‚Üícreation: state transitions, plan refinements, or both.
   911‚Üí
   912‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
   913‚Üí
   914‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
   915‚Üí* {jjda_file}
   916‚Üí
   917‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   918‚Üí* {jjdt_coronet}
   919‚Üí(positional)
   920‚Üí
   921‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
   922‚Üí* {jjda_state}
   923‚Üí‚Äî if absent, inherits from previous
   924‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   925‚Üí
   926‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_conditional‚üß
   927‚Üí* {jjda_direction}
   928‚Üí‚Äî required if
   929‚Üí{jjda_state}
   930‚Üíis
   931‚Üí{jjdpe_primed};
   932‚Üíforbidden if
   933‚Üí{jjda_state}
   934‚Üíis provided and not
   935‚Üí{jjdpe_primed};
   936‚Üíignored if
   937‚Üí{jjda_state}
   938‚Üíis absent (inherits with state)
   939‚Üí
   940‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
   941‚Üí* stdin ‚Äî
   942‚Üí{jjdkm_text}
   943‚Üícontent; if absent, inherits from previous
   944‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   945‚Üí
   946‚Üí{jjds_stdout} None
   947‚Üí
   948‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
   949‚Üí
   950‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   951‚Üí
   952‚Üí. Read
   953‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   954‚Üífrom file
   955‚Üí. Extract parent
   956‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   957‚Üífrom
   958‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   959‚Üí(first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
   960‚Üí. Verify
   961‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   962‚Üíexists
   963‚Üí. Verify
   964‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   965‚Üíexists with given
   966‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   967‚Üí. Read current
   968‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   969‚Üífrom `tacks[0]`
   970‚Üí. Determine new state:
   971‚Üí.. If
   972‚Üí{jjda_state}
   973‚Üíprovided: use that value
   974‚Üí.. Otherwise: inherit from previous
   975‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   976‚Üí. Determine new direction:
   977‚Üí.. If
   978‚Üí{jjda_state}
   979‚Üíis
   980‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}:
   981‚Üíuse
   982‚Üí{jjda_direction}
   983‚Üíargument (required)
   984‚Üí.. If
   985‚Üí{jjda_state}
   986‚Üíis absent and previous state is
   987‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}:
   988‚Üíinherit from previous
   989‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   990‚Üí.. Otherwise: omit
   991‚Üí{jjdkm_direction}
   992‚Üí. Determine new text:
   993‚Üí.. If stdin provided: use stdin content
   994‚Üí.. Otherwise: inherit from previous
   995‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   996‚Üí. Create new
   997‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   998‚Üíwith:
   999‚Üí.. {jjdkm_refined}:
  1000‚Üícurrent timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
  1001‚Üí.. {jjdkm_state}:
  1002‚Üídetermined above
  1003‚Üí.. {jjdkm_text}:
  1004‚Üídetermined above
  1005‚Üí.. {jjdkm_direction}:
  1006‚Üídetermined above (if applicable)
  1007‚Üí. Prepend new
  1008‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
  1009‚Üíto `tacks` array (position 0)
  1010‚Üí. Write transformed
  1011‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1012‚Üíto file (atomic)
  1013‚Üí
  1014‚Üí=== Read Operations
  1015‚Üí
  1016‚Üí[[jjdo_validate]]
  1017‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1018‚Üí==== {jjdo_validate}
  1019‚Üí
  1020‚ÜíCheck that
  1021‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1022‚ÜíJSON conforms to schema.
  1023‚Üí
  1024‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1025‚Üí
  1026‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
  1027‚Üí* {jjda_file}
  1028‚Üí
  1029‚Üí{jjds_stdout} None
  1030‚Üí
  1031‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1032‚Üí
  1033‚Üí*Validation Rules:*
  1034‚Üí
  1035‚Üí. Root must be JSON object with
  1036‚Üí{jjdgm_seed}
  1037‚Üí(2 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`)
  1038‚Üí. Root must have `heats` object
  1039‚Üí. Each
  1040‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1041‚Üíkey must match `‚Ç£[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2}`
  1042‚Üí. Each
  1043‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1044‚Üímust have:
  1045‚Üí{jjdhm_silks}
  1046‚Üí(non-empty kebab-case),
  1047‚Üí{jjdhm_created}
  1048‚Üí(YYMMDD),
  1049‚Üí{jjdhm_status}
  1050‚Üí({jjdhe_current}
  1051‚Üíor
  1052‚Üí{jjdhe_retired}),
  1053‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
  1054‚Üí(array),
  1055‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}
  1056‚Üí(3 URL-safe base64 characters matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{3}`),
  1057‚Üí`paces` (object)
  1058‚Üí. {jjdhm_order}
  1059‚Üíarray and `paces` object must have identical key sets
  1060‚Üí. Each
  1061‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
  1062‚Üíkey must match `‚Ç¢[A-Za-z0-9_-]{5}` and embed parent
  1063‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1064‚Üíidentity (first 2 base64 chars after prefix)
  1065‚Üí. Each
  1066‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
  1067‚Üímust have:
  1068‚Üí{jjdpm_silks}
  1069‚Üí(non-empty kebab-case),
  1070‚Üí`tacks` (non-empty array)
  1071‚Üí. Each
  1072‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
  1073‚Üímust have:
  1074‚Üí{jjdkm_refined}
  1075‚Üí(YYMMDD-HHMM),
  1076‚Üí{jjdkm_state}
  1077‚Üí({jjdpe_rough},
  1078‚Üí{jjdpe_primed},
  1079‚Üí{jjdpe_complete},
  1080‚Üíor
  1081‚Üí{jjdpe_abandoned}),
  1082‚Üí{jjdkm_text}
  1083‚Üí(non-empty string)
  1084‚Üí. If
  1085‚Üí{jjdkm_state}
  1086‚Üíis
  1087‚Üí{jjdpe_primed},
  1088‚Üí{jjdkm_direction}
  1089‚Üímust be present (non-empty string); otherwise
  1090‚Üí{jjdkm_direction}
  1091‚Üímust be absent
  1092‚Üí
  1093‚Üí[[jjdo_muster]]
  1094‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1095‚Üí==== {jjdo_muster}
  1096‚Üí
  1097‚ÜíList all
  1098‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1099‚Üíwith summary information.
  1100‚Üí
  1101‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1102‚Üí
  1103‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
  1104‚Üí* {jjda_file}
  1105‚Üí
  1106‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
  1107‚Üí* {jjda_status}
  1108‚Üí‚Äî filter to show only
  1109‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1110‚Üíwith this status; if absent, show all
  1111‚Üí
  1112‚Üí{jjds_stdout} Tab-separated values, one line per
  1113‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}:
  1114‚Üí`{jjdt_firemark}<TAB>{jjdt_silks}<TAB>{jjdhm_status}<TAB><pace_count>`
  1115‚Üí
  1116‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1117‚Üí
  1118‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1119‚Üí
  1120‚Üí. Read
  1121‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1122‚Üífrom file
  1123‚Üí. Iterate over `heats` object entries
  1124‚Üí. If
  1125‚Üí{jjda_status}
  1126‚Üíprovided, skip
  1127‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
  1128‚Üínot matching filter
  1129‚Üí. For each included
  1130‚Üí{jjdhr_heat},
  1131‚Üíoutput TSV line:
  1132‚Üí{jjdt_firemark},
  1133‚Üí{jjdhm_silks},
  1134‚Üí{jjdhm_status},
  1135‚Üícount of
  1136‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1137‚Üíin `paces` object
  1138‚Üí. Output lines in arbitrary order (JSON object iteration order)
  1139‚Üí
  1140‚Üí[[jjdo_retire]]
  1141‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1142‚Üí==== {jjdo_retire}
  1143‚Üí
  1144‚ÜíExtract complete
  1145‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1146‚Üídata for archival trophy.
  1147‚ÜíIncludes paddock content and full steeplechase history from git.
  1148‚ÜíUse before squash merge to preserve session history.
  1149‚Üí
  1150‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1151‚Üí
  1152‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
  1153‚Üí* {jjda_file}
  1154‚Üí
  1155‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1156‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
  1157‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî target
  1158‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1159‚Üí
  1160‚Üí{jjds_stdout} JSON object containing complete archive:
  1161‚Üí[source,json]
  1162‚Üí----
  1163‚Üí{
  1164‚Üí  "firemark": "‚Ç£XX",
  1165‚Üí  "silks": "...",
  1166‚Üí  "created": "YYMMDD",
  1167‚Üí  "status": "current|retired",
  1168‚Üí  "paddock_file": ".claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md",
  1169‚Üí  "paddock_content": "...",
  1170‚Üí  "paces": [
  1171‚Üí    {
  1172‚Üí      "coronet": "‚Ç¢XXXXX",
  1173‚Üí      "silks": "...",
  1174‚Üí      "tacks": [
  1175‚Üí        {
  1176‚Üí          "ts": "YYMMDD-HHMM",
  1177‚Üí          "state": "...",
  1178‚Üí          "text": "...",
  1179‚Üí          "direction": "..."
  1180‚Üí        }
  1181‚Üí      ]
  1182‚Üí    }
  1183‚Üí  ],
  1184‚Üí  "steeplechase": [
  1185‚Üí    {
  1186‚Üí      "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  1187‚Üí      "pace_silks": "...",
  1188‚Üí      "marker": null,
  1189‚Üí      "subject": "commit message"
  1190‚Üí    }
  1191‚Üí  ]
  1192‚Üí}
  1193‚Üí----
  1194‚ÜíThe `paces` array is ordered per
  1195‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
  1196‚Üíwith full
  1197‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
  1198‚Üíhistory for each pace.
  1199‚ÜíField `direction` appears only on
  1200‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
  1201‚Üíwith state
  1202‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}.
  1203‚ÜíThe `steeplechase` array contains all session history entries (from
  1204‚Üí{jjdo_rein}).
  1205‚Üí
  1206‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1207‚Üí
  1208‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1209‚Üí
  1210‚Üí. Read
  1211‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1212‚Üífrom file
  1213‚Üí. Verify
  1214‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1215‚Üíexists with given
  1216‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
  1217‚Üí. Extract
  1218‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1219‚Üímetadata:
  1220‚Üí{jjdt_firemark},
  1221‚Üí{jjdhm_silks},
  1222‚Üí{jjdhm_created},
  1223‚Üí{jjdhm_status}
  1224‚Üí. Output `paddock_file` from
  1225‚Üí{jjdhm_paddock}
  1226‚Üí. Read paddock file contents, output as `paddock_content`
  1227‚Üí. Build `paces` array by walking
  1228‚Üí{jjdhm_order}:
  1229‚Üí.. For each
  1230‚Üí{jjdt_coronet},
  1231‚Üíinclude
  1232‚Üí{jjdpm_silks}
  1233‚Üíand full `tacks` array (all history)
  1234‚Üí. Call
  1235‚Üí{jjdo_rein}
  1236‚Üíinternally to get steeplechase entries
  1237‚Üí. Output JSON object with all fields
  1238‚Üí
  1239‚Üí[[jjdo_saddle]]
  1240‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1241‚Üí==== {jjdo_saddle}
  1242‚Üí
  1243‚ÜíReturn context needed to saddle up on a
  1244‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}:
  1245‚Üíthe current
  1246‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
  1247‚Üí(first in
  1248‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
  1249‚Üíwhose state is
  1250‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}
  1251‚Üíor
  1252‚Üí{jjdpe_primed})
  1253‚Üíand its
  1254‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
  1255‚Üídetails.
  1256‚Üí
  1257‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1258‚Üí
  1259‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
  1260‚Üí* {jjda_file}
  1261‚Üí
  1262‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1263‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
  1264‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî target
  1265‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1266‚Üí
  1267‚Üí{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:
  1268‚Üí[source,json]
  1269‚Üí----
  1270‚Üí{
  1271‚Üí  "heat_silks": "...",
  1272‚Üí  "paddock_file": ".claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md",
  1273‚Üí  "paddock_content": "...",
  1274‚Üí  "pace_coronet": "‚Ç¢XXXXX",
  1275‚Üí  "pace_silks": "...",
  1276‚Üí  "pace_state": "rough|primed",
  1277‚Üí  "tack_text": "...",
  1278‚Üí  "tack_direction": "..."
  1279‚Üí}
  1280‚Üí----
  1281‚ÜíFields `pace_coronet` through `tack_direction` are absent if no actionable pace exists.
  1282‚ÜíField `tack_direction` is present only when `pace_state` is
  1283‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}.
  1284‚ÜíField `paddock_content` contains the full text of the paddock file.
  1285‚Üí
  1286‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1287‚Üí
  1288‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1289‚Üí
  1290‚Üí. Read
  1291‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1292‚Üífrom file
  1293‚Üí. Verify
  1294‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1295‚Üíexists with given
  1296‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
  1297‚Üí. Output `heat_silks` from
  1298‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1299‚Üí. Output `paddock_file` from
  1300‚Üí{jjdhm_paddock}
  1301‚Üí. Read paddock file contents, output as `paddock_content`
  1302‚Üí. Walk
  1303‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
  1304‚Üíarray in sequence
  1305‚Üí. For each
  1306‚Üí{jjdt_coronet},
  1307‚Üícheck `paces[coronet].tacks[0].state`
  1308‚Üí. If state is
  1309‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}
  1310‚Üíor
  1311‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}:
  1312‚Üí.. Output `pace_coronet`,
  1313‚Üí`pace_silks`,
  1314‚Üí`pace_state`
  1315‚Üí.. Output `tack_text` from `tacks[0].text`
  1316‚Üí.. If state is
  1317‚Üí{jjdpe_primed},
  1318‚Üíoutput `tack_direction` from `tacks[0].direction`
  1319‚Üí.. Stop walking (first match wins)
  1320‚Üí. If no actionable pace found, output only `heat_silks`, `paddock_file`, and `paddock_content`
  1321‚Üí
  1322‚Üí[[jjdo_parade]]
  1323‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1324‚Üí==== {jjdo_parade}
  1325‚Üí
  1326‚ÜíDisplay comprehensive
  1327‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1328‚Üístatus for project review.
  1329‚ÜíShows paddock, all
  1330‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
  1331‚Üíin order, with tack details for actionable paces.
  1332‚Üí
  1333‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1334‚Üí
  1335‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß
  1336‚Üí* {jjda_file}
  1337‚Üí
  1338‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1339‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
  1340‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî target
  1341‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1342‚Üí
  1343‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
  1344‚Üí* `--full` ‚Äî include tack details for
  1345‚Üí{jjdpe_complete}
  1346‚Üíand
  1347‚Üí{jjdpe_abandoned}
  1348‚Üípaces (default: silks only)
  1349‚Üí
  1350‚Üí{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:
  1351‚Üí[source,json]
  1352‚Üí----
  1353‚Üí{
  1354‚Üí  "heat_silks": "...",
  1355‚Üí  "heat_created": "YYMMDD",
  1356‚Üí  "heat_status": "current|retired",
  1357‚Üí  "paddock_file": ".claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md",
  1358‚Üí  "paddock_content": "...",
  1359‚Üí  "paces": [
  1360‚Üí    {
  1361‚Üí      "coronet": "‚Ç¢XXXXX",
  1362‚Üí      "silks": "...",
  1363‚Üí      "state": "rough|primed",
  1364‚Üí      "tack_text": "...",
  1365‚Üí      "tack_direction": "..."
  1366‚Üí    },
  1367‚Üí    {
  1368‚Üí      "coronet": "‚Ç¢YYYYY",
  1369‚Üí      "silks": "...",
  1370‚Üí      "state": "complete"
  1371‚Üí    }
  1372‚Üí  ]
  1373‚Üí}
  1374‚Üí----
  1375‚ÜíFor
  1376‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}
  1377‚Üíor
  1378‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}
  1379‚Üípaces: includes `tack_text` (and `tack_direction` if primed).
  1380‚ÜíFor
  1381‚Üí{jjdpe_complete}
  1382‚Üíor
  1383‚Üí{jjdpe_abandoned}
  1384‚Üípaces: includes only `silks` and `state` unless `--full` flag is set.
  1385‚ÜíPaces ordered per
  1386‚Üí{jjdhm_order}.
  1387‚Üí
  1388‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1389‚Üí
  1390‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1391‚Üí
  1392‚Üí. Read
  1393‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1394‚Üífrom file
  1395‚Üí. Verify
  1396‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1397‚Üíexists with given
  1398‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
  1399‚Üí. Output heat metadata: `heat_silks`, `heat_created`, `heat_status`
  1400‚Üí. Output `paddock_file` from
  1401‚Üí{jjdhm_paddock}
  1402‚Üí. Read paddock file contents, output as `paddock_content`
  1403‚Üí. Build `paces` array by walking
  1404‚Üí{jjdhm_order}:
  1405‚Üí.. For each
  1406‚Üí{jjdt_coronet},
  1407‚Üíread `tacks[0]` to get current state
  1408‚Üí.. Output `coronet`, `silks`, `state`
  1409‚Üí.. If state is
  1410‚Üí{jjdpe_rough}
  1411‚Üíor
  1412‚Üí{jjdpe_primed}:
  1413‚Üíoutput `tack_text` (and `tack_direction` if primed)
  1414‚Üí.. If state is
  1415‚Üí{jjdpe_complete}
  1416‚Üíor
  1417‚Üí{jjdpe_abandoned}
  1418‚Üíand `--full` flag:
  1419‚Üíoutput `tack_text`
  1420‚Üí. Output JSON
  1421‚Üí
  1422‚Üí=== Steeplechase Operations
  1423‚Üí
  1424‚ÜíThe steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  1425‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1426‚ÜíJSON.
  1427‚ÜíCommit messages follow patterns that
  1428‚Üí{jjdo_rein}
  1429‚Üícan parse to reconstruct session history.
  1430‚Üí
  1431‚Üí==== Commit Message Patterns
  1432‚Üí
  1433‚Üí*Standard commit* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  1434‚Üí----
  1435‚Üí[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX/pace-silks] commit message
  1436‚Üí
  1437‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  1438‚Üí----
  1439‚Üí
  1440‚Üí* `BRAND` ‚Äî repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time
  1441‚Üí* `‚Ç£XX` ‚Äî {jjdt_firemark} of the active heat
  1442‚Üí* `pace-silks` ‚Äî {jjdt_silks} of the current pace
  1443‚Üí
  1444‚Üí*Steeplechase marker* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  1445‚Üí----
  1446‚Üí[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX] MARKER: description
  1447‚Üí
  1448‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  1449‚Üí----
  1450‚Üí
  1451‚ÜíMarker types:
  1452‚Üí
  1453‚Üí* `APPROACH` ‚Äî proposed approach for a pace, recorded before work begins
  1454‚Üí* `WRAP` ‚Äî pace completion summary
  1455‚Üí* `FLY` ‚Äî autonomous execution began (primed pace)
  1456‚Üí* `DISCUSSION` ‚Äî significant decision or design discussion
  1457‚Üí
  1458‚Üí[[jjdo_rein]]
  1459‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1460‚Üí==== {jjdo_rein}
  1461‚Üí
  1462‚ÜíParse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
  1463‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
  1464‚Üí
  1465‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1466‚Üí
  1467‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1468‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
  1469‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî target
  1470‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1471‚Üí
  1472‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
  1473‚Üí* `--limit` ‚Äî maximum entries to return (default: 50)
  1474‚Üí
  1475‚Üí{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
  1476‚Üí[source,json]
  1477‚Üí----
  1478‚Üí[
  1479‚Üí  {
  1480‚Üí    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  1481‚Üí    "pace_silks": "...",
  1482‚Üí    "marker": null,
  1483‚Üí    "subject": "commit message"
  1484‚Üí  },
  1485‚Üí  {
  1486‚Üí    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  1487‚Üí    "pace_silks": null,
  1488‚Üí    "marker": "APPROACH",
  1489‚Üí    "subject": "description"
  1490‚Üí  }
  1491‚Üí]
  1492‚Üí----
  1493‚ÜíField `pace_silks` is present for standard commits, `marker` for chalk entries.
  1494‚ÜíEntries ordered newest first.
  1495‚Üí
  1496‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1497‚Üí
  1498‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1499‚Üí
  1500‚Üí. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^\[jj:BRAND\]\[‚Ç£XX" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  1501‚Üí. Parse each line: extract timestamp, determine if standard commit or marker
  1502‚Üí. For standard commits: extract pace-silks from `[‚Ç£XX/pace-silks]`
  1503‚Üí. For markers: extract marker type and description
  1504‚Üí. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
  1505‚Üí. Output JSON
  1506‚Üí
  1507‚Üí[[jjdo_notch]]
  1508‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1509‚Üí==== {jjdo_notch}
  1510‚Üí
  1511‚ÜíCreate a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.
  1512‚Üí
  1513‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1514‚Üí
  1515‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1516‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
  1517‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî active
  1518‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1519‚Üí
  1520‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1521‚Üí* `--pace` ‚Äî {jjdt_silks} of the current pace
  1522‚Üí
  1523‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
  1524‚Üí* `--message` ‚Äî commit message; if absent, invoke `claude --print` to generate from diff
  1525‚Üí
  1526‚Üí{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  1527‚Üí
  1528‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1529‚Üí
  1530‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1531‚Üí
  1532‚Üí. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1533‚Üí. Stage changes (`git add -u`)
  1534‚Üí. Run size guard check
  1535‚Üí. If `--message` absent:
  1536‚Üí.. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
  1537‚Üí.. Use response as message
  1538‚Üí. Format full message: `[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX/pace-silks] message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  1539‚Üí. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  1540‚Üí. Release lock
  1541‚Üí. Output commit hash
  1542‚Üí
  1543‚Üí[[jjdo_chalk]]
  1544‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1545‚Üí==== {jjdo_chalk}
  1546‚Üí
  1547‚ÜíCreate an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.
  1548‚Üí
  1549‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1550‚Üí
  1551‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1552‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
  1553‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî active
  1554‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1555‚Üí
  1556‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1557‚Üí* `--marker` ‚Äî marker type: `APPROACH`, `WRAP`, `FLY`, or `DISCUSSION`
  1558‚Üí
  1559‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1560‚Üí* `--description` ‚Äî marker description text
  1561‚Üí
  1562‚Üí{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  1563‚Üí
  1564‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1565‚Üí
  1566‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1567‚Üí
  1568‚Üí. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1569‚Üí. Format message: `[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX] MARKER: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  1570‚Üí. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
  1571‚Üí. Release lock
  1572‚Üí. Output commit hash
  1573‚Üí

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

= Job Jockey Gallops Data Model
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// jjdt_:  Types (scalar value types with validation rules)
// jjdgr_: Gallops record
// jjdgm_: Gallops members
// jjdhr_: Heat record
// jjdhm_: Heat members
// jjdhe_: Heat enum values (status)
// jjdpr_: Pace record
// jjdpm_: Pace members
// jjdkr_: Tack record
// jjdkm_: Tack members
// jjdpe_: Tack state enum values
// jjdo_:  Operations
// jjds_:  Section headers (operation documentation structure)
// jjdz_:  Serialization concerns
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

// Types
:jjdt_coronet:    <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet>>
:jjdt_coronet_p:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet's>>
:jjdt_coronet_s:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronets>>
:jjdt_firemark:   <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark>>
:jjdt_firemark_p: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark's>>
:jjdt_firemark_s: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemarks>>
:jjdt_silks:      <<jjdt_silks,Silks>>

// Gallops record and members
:jjdgm_seed:        <<jjdgm_seed,next_heat_seed>>
:jjdgr_gallops:     <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops>>
:jjdgr_gallops_p:   <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops'>>

// Heat record and members
:jjdhe_current:   <<jjdhe_current,current>>
:jjdhe_retired:   <<jjdhe_retired,retired>>
:jjdhm_created:   <<jjdhm_created,creation_time>>
:jjdhm_firemark:  <<jjdhm_firemark,firemark>>
:jjdhm_order:     <<jjdhm_order,order>>
:jjdhm_paddock:   <<jjdhm_paddock,paddock_file>>
:jjdhm_seed:      <<jjdhm_seed,next_pace_seed>>
:jjdhm_silks:     <<jjdhm_silks,silks>>
:jjdhm_status:    <<jjdhm_status,status>>
:jjdhr_heat:      <<jjdhr_heat,Heat>>
:jjdhr_heat_p:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heat's>>
:jjdhr_heat_s:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heats>>

// Pace record and members
:jjdpm_coronet:   <<jjdpm_coronet,coronet>>
:jjdpm_silks:     <<jjdpm_silks,silks>>
:jjdpr_pace:      <<jjdpr_pace,Pace>>
:jjdpr_pace_p:    <<jjdpr_pace,Pace's>>
:jjdpr_pace_s:    <<jjdpr_pace,Paces>>

// Pace/Tack state enum values (used in Tack)
:jjdpe_abandoned: <<jjdpe_abandoned,abandoned>>
:jjdpe_complete:  <<jjdpe_complete,complete>>
:jjdpe_primed:    <<jjdpe_primed,primed>>
:jjdpe_rough:     <<jjdpe_rough,rough>>

// Tack record and members
:jjdkm_direction: <<jjdkm_direction,direction>>
:jjdkm_refined:   <<jjdkm_refined,ts>>
:jjdkm_state:     <<jjdkm_state,state>>
:jjdkm_text:      <<jjdkm_text,text>>
:jjdkr_tack:      <<jjdkr_tack,Tack>>
:jjdkr_tack_p:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tack's>>
:jjdkr_tack_s:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tacks>>

// Arguments
:jjda_created:        <<jjda_created,--created>>
:jjda_direction:      <<jjda_direction,--direction>>
:jjda_file:           <<jjda_file,--file>>
:jjda_silks:          <<jjda_silks,--silks>>
:jjda_state:          <<jjda_state,--state>>
:jjda_status:         <<jjda_status,--status>>

// CLI executor
:jjdx_vvx:   <<jjdx_vvx,vvx>>
:jjdx_cli:   <<jjdx_cli,jjx>>

// Operations
:jjdo_canter:         <<jjdo_canter,jjx_canter>>
:jjdo_chalk:          <<jjdo_chalk,jjx_chalk>>
:jjdo_muster:         <<jjdo_muster,jjx_muster>>
:jjdo_nominate:       <<jjdo_nominate,jjx_nominate>>
:jjdo_notch:          <<jjdo_notch,jjx_notch>>
:jjdo_rail:           <<jjdo_rail,jjx_rail>>
:jjdo_rein:           <<jjdo_rein,jjx_rein>>
:jjdo_retire:         <<jjdo_retire,jjx_retire>>
:jjdo_saddle:         <<jjdo_saddle,jjx_saddle>>
:jjdo_slate:          <<jjdo_slate,jjx_slate>>
:jjdo_tally:          <<jjdo_tally,jjx_tally>>
:jjdo_validate:       <<jjdo_validate,jjx_validate>>

// Section headers
:jjds_arguments:        <<jjds_arguments,Arguments:>>
:jjds_behavior:         <<jjds_behavior,Behavior:>>
:jjds_exit_enumerated:  <<jjds_exit_enumerated,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_exit_uniform:     <<jjds_exit_uniform,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_stdout:           <<jjds_stdout,Stdout:>>

// Serialization
:jjdz_charset:  <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
:jjdz_encoding: <<jjdz_encoding,Identity Encoding>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).
Each
{jjdpr_pace}
maintains a history of
{jjdkr_tack_s}
(plan refinements).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
The internet is full of bad bash.
Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

The
{jjdkr_tack}
history within each
{jjdpr_pace}
exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
Git has the full history, but
{jjdkr_tack_s}
let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
prepend to position 0 rather than append.
This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.

The pattern:
[source,bash]
----
lock_acquire
vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
lock_release
----

This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.

=== Identity Encoding

{jjdt_firemark}
(`‚Ç£` prefix) and
{jjdt_coronet}
(`‚Ç¢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.

=== Horse Racing Vocabulary

Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.

* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `‚Ç£`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `‚Ç¢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)

== Types

[[jjdt_firemark]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
{jjdt_firemark}::
The identity of a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç£AB`).
The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `‚Ç£` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_coronet]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
{jjdt_coronet}::
The identity of a
{jjdpr_pace}.
Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç¢ABCDE`).
Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
{jjdhr_heat_p}
encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `‚Ç¢` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_silks]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
{jjdt_silks}::
A kebab-case display name.
Used for human-readable identification of
{jjdhr_heat_s}
and
{jjdpr_pace_s}.
Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`

== Records

=== {jjdgr_gallops}

[[jjdgr_gallops]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdgr_gallops}::
The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.

==== Members

* [[jjdgm_seed]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdgm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_firemark}
to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_nominate}.

=== {jjdhr_heat}

[[jjdhr_heat]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdhr_heat}::
A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
Contains zero or more
{jjdpr_pace_s}
representing discrete actions.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_firemark}.

==== Members

* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_firemark}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
primary key.
Type:
{jjdt_firemark}.
Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.

* [[jjdhm_silks]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_silks}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.

* [[jjdhm_created]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_created}
records when the
{jjdhr_heat}
was nominated.
Format: YYMMDD.

* [[jjdhm_status]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_status}
indicates the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
lifecycle state.
Values:
{jjdhe_current}
or
{jjdhe_retired}.

* [[jjdhm_order]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated‚üß
{jjdhm_order}
lists
{jjdt_coronet_s}
in execution sequence.
May be reordered via
{jjdo_rail}.

* [[jjdhm_seed]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_coronet}
index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_slate}.

* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_paddock}
is the path to the paddock file containing
{jjdhr_heat}
context and background.
Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
{jjdt_firemark}
(without `‚Ç£` prefix).
Created by
{jjdo_nominate}.

==== Status Values

[[jjdhe_current]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdhe_current}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is active and accepting work.

[[jjdhe_retired]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdhe_retired}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is complete and archived.

=== {jjdpr_pace}

[[jjdpr_pace]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdpr_pace}::
A discrete action within a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Contains one or more
{jjdkr_tack_s}
representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_coronet}.

==== Members

* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdpm_coronet}
is the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
globally unique identity.
Type:
{jjdt_coronet}.
Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.

* [[jjdpm_silks]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdpm_silks}
is the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.

=== {jjdkr_tack}

[[jjdkr_tack]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdkr_tack}::
A snapshot of
{jjdpr_pace}
state and plan at a point in time.
{jjdkr_tack_s}
form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
Position 0 is the most recent; older
{jjdkr_tack_s}
follow.
New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
The current
{jjdpr_pace}
state is always
`tacks[0].state`.

==== Members

* [[jjdkm_refined]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdkm_refined}
records when this
{jjdkr_tack}
was added.
Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.

* [[jjdkm_state]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdkm_state}
indicates the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
progress at this snapshot.
Values:
{jjdpe_rough},
{jjdpe_primed},
{jjdpe_complete},
or
{jjdpe_abandoned}.

* [[jjdkm_text]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdkm_text}
contains the plan specification.
Non-empty string describing what the
{jjdpr_pace}
should accomplish.

* [[jjdkm_direction]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional‚üß
{jjdkm_direction}
contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
Required when
{jjdkm_state}
is
{jjdpe_primed};
forbidden otherwise.
Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.

==== State Values

[[jjdpe_rough]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdpe_rough}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
needs clarification before work can proceed.
When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_rough}
pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.

[[jjdpe_primed]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdpe_primed}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
is ready for autonomous execution.
The
{jjdkm_direction}
field contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_primed}
pace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.

[[jjdpe_complete]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdpe_complete}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}

== Serialization

[[jjdz_charset]]
{jjdz_charset}::
The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 ¬ß5):
`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.

[[jjdz_encoding]]
{jjdz_encoding}::
The algorithm for encoding
{jjdt_firemark}
and
{jjdt_coronet}
values.
+
*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
+
. Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters
. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `‚Ç£` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
. To decode `‚Ç£XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
+
*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
+
. Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters
. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `‚Ç¢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
. To decode `‚Ç¢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
+
*Input flexibility:*
+
CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
Output always includes the prefix (`‚Ç£` or `‚Ç¢`).

== CLI

[[jjdx_vvx]]

{jjdx_vvx}::
The Voce Viva executor.
Local reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.

[[jjdx_cli]]

{jjdx_cli}::
The Job Jockey command group within
{jjdx_vvx}.
Invoked as `vvx jjx`.
All operations are subcommands of
{jjdx_cli}.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.

== Operations

Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access (read, transform, atomic write).
Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
{jjda_file}.

=== Steeplechase Operations

The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
{jjdgr_gallops}
JSON.
Commit messages follow patterns that
{jjdo_rein}
can parse to reconstruct session history.

==== Commit Message Patterns

*Standard commit* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
----
[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX/pace-silks] commit message

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

* `BRAND` ‚Äî repository identifier baked into arcanum at install time
* `‚Ç£XX` ‚Äî {jjdt_firemark} of the active heat
* `pace-silks` ‚Äî {jjdt_silks} of the current pace

*Steeplechase marker* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
----
[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX] MARKER: description

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Marker types:

* `APPROACH` ‚Äî proposed approach for a pace, recorded before work begins
* `WRAP` ‚Äî pace completion summary
* `FLY` ‚Äî autonomous execution began (primed pace)
* `DISCUSSION` ‚Äî significant decision or design discussion

[[jjdo_rein]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
==== {jjdo_rein}

Parse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
{jjdhr_heat}.

{jjds_arguments}

// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) ‚Äî target
{jjdhr_heat}

// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
* `--limit` ‚Äî maximum entries to return (default: 50)

{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
[source,json]
----
[
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "pace_silks": "...",
    "marker": null,
    "subject": "commit message"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
    "pace_silks": null,
    "marker": "APPROACH",
    "subject": "description"
  }
]
----
Field `pace_silks` is present for standard commits, `marker` for chalk entries.
Entries ordered newest first.

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^\[jj:BRAND\]\[‚Ç£XX" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
. Parse each line: extract timestamp, determine if standard commit or marker
. For standard commits: extract pace-silks from `[‚Ç£XX/pace-silks]`
. For markers: extract marker type and description
. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
. Output JSON

[[jjdo_notch]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
==== {jjdo_notch}

Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.

{jjds_arguments}

// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) ‚Äî active
{jjdhr_heat}

// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
* `--pace` ‚Äî {jjdt_silks} of the current pace

// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
* `--message` ‚Äî commit message; if absent, invoke `claude --print` to generate from diff

{jjds_stdout} Commit hash

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Stage changes (`git add -u`)
. Run size guard check
. If `--message` absent:
.. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
.. Use response as message
. Format full message: `[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX/pace-silks] message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
. Run `git commit -m "..."`
. Release lock
. Output commit hash

[[jjdo_chalk]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
==== {jjdo_chalk}

Create an empty commit marking a steeplechase event.

{jjds_arguments}

// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
* {jjdt_firemark}
(positional) ‚Äî active
{jjdhr_heat}

// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
* `--marker` ‚Äî marker type: `APPROACH`, `WRAP`, `FLY`, or `DISCUSSION`

// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
* `--description` ‚Äî marker description text

{jjds_stdout} Commit hash

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

{jjds_behavior}

. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
. Format message: `[jj:BRAND][‚Ç£XX] MARKER: description\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
. Run `git commit --allow-empty -m "..."`
. Release lock
. Output commit hash

= Job Jockey Gallops Data Model
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// jjdt_:  Types (scalar value types with validation rules)
// jjdgr_: Gallops record
// jjdgm_: Gallops members
// jjdhr_: Heat record
// jjdhm_: Heat members
// jjdhe_: Heat enum values (status)
// jjdpr_: Pace record
// jjdpm_: Pace members
// jjdkr_: Tack record
// jjdkm_: Tack members
// jjdpe_: Tack state enum values
// jjdo_:  Operations
// jjds_:  Section headers (operation documentation structure)
// jjdz_:  Serialization concerns
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

// Types
:jjdt_coronet:    <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet>>
:jjdt_coronet_p:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronet's>>
:jjdt_coronet_s:  <<jjdt_coronet,Coronets>>
:jjdt_firemark:   <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark>>
:jjdt_firemark_p: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemark's>>
:jjdt_firemark_s: <<jjdt_firemark,Firemarks>>
:jjdt_silks:      <<jjdt_silks,Silks>>

// Gallops record and members
:jjdgm_seed:        <<jjdgm_seed,next_heat_seed>>
:jjdgr_gallops:     <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops>>
:jjdgr_gallops_p:   <<jjdgr_gallops,Gallops'>>

// Heat record and members
:jjdhe_current:   <<jjdhe_current,current>>
:jjdhe_retired:   <<jjdhe_retired,retired>>

:jjdhm_firemark:  <<jjdhm_firemark,firemark>>
:jjdhm_order:     <<jjdhm_order,order>>
:jjdhm_paddock:   <<jjdhm_paddock,paddock_file>>
:jjdhm_seed:      <<jjdhm_seed,next_pace_seed>>
:jjdhm_silks:     <<jjdhm_silks,silks>>
:jjdhm_status:    <<jjdhm_status,status>>
:jjdhr_heat:      <<jjdhr_heat,Heat>>
:jjdhr_heat_p:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heat's>>
:jjdhr_heat_s:    <<jjdhr_heat,Heats>>

// Pace record and members
:jjdpm_coronet:   <<jjdpm_coronet,coronet>>

:jjdpr_pace_p:    <<jjdpr_pace,Pace's>>

// Pace/Tack state enum values (used in Tack)
:jjdpe_abandoned: <<jjdpe_abandoned,abandoned>>

// Tack record and members
:jjdkm_direction: <<jjdkm_direction,direction>>

:jjdkr_tack:      <<jjdkr_tack,Tack>>
:jjdkr_tack_p:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tack's>>
:jjdkr_tack_s:    <<jjdkr_tack,Tacks>>

// Arguments
:jjda_created:        <<jjda_created,--created>>
:jjda_direction:      <<jjda_direction,--direction>>
:jjda_file:           <<jjda_file,--file>>
:jjda_silks:          <<jjda_silks,--silks>>

:jjda_status:         <<jjda_status,--status>>

// CLI executor
:jjdx_vvx:   <<jjdx_vvx,vvx>>

// Operations
:jjdo_canter:         <<jjdo_canter,jjx_canter>>
:jjdo_chalk:          <<jjdo_chalk,jjx_chalk>>
:jjdo_muster:         <<jjdo_muster,jjx_muster>>
:jjdo_nominate:       <<jjdo_nominate,jjx_nominate>>
:jjdo_notch:          <<jjdo_notch,jjx_notch>>
:jjdo_rail:           <<jjdo_rail,jjx_rail>>
:jjdo_rein:           <<jjdo_rein,jjx_rein>>
:jjdo_retire:         <<jjdo_retire,jjx_retire>>
:jjdo_saddle:         <<jjdo_saddle,jjx_saddle>>
:jjdo_slate:          <<jjdo_slate,jjx_slate>>
:jjdo_tally:          <<jjdo_tally,jjx_tally>>

// Section headers
:jjds_arguments:        <<jjds_arguments,Arguments:>>
:jjds_behavior:         <<jjds_behavior,Behavior:>>
:jjds_exit_enumerated:  <<jjds_exit_enumerated,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_exit_uniform:     <<jjds_exit_uniform,Exit Status:>>
:jjds_stdout:           <<jjds_stdout,Stdout:>>

// Serialization
:jjdz_charset:  <<jjdz_charset,Charset>>
:jjdz_encoding: <<jjdz_encoding,Identity Encoding>>

// end::mapping-section[]

== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).
Each
{jjdpr_pace}
maintains a history of
{jjdkr_tack_s}
(plan refinements).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
The internet is full of bad bash.
Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

The
{jjdkr_tack}
history within each
{jjdpr_pace}
exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
Git has the full history, but
{jjdkr_tack_s}
let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
prepend to position 0 rather than append.
This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.

The pattern:
[source,bash]
----
lock_acquire
vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
lock_release
----

This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.

=== Identity Encoding

{jjdt_firemark}
(`‚Ç£` prefix) and
{jjdt_coronet}
(`‚Ç¢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.

=== Horse Racing Vocabulary

Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.

* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `‚Ç£`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `‚Ç¢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)

== Types

[[jjdt_firemark]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
{jjdt_firemark}::
The identity of a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç£AB`).
The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `‚Ç£` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_coronet]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
{jjdt_coronet}::
The identity of a
{jjdpr_pace}.
Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç¢ABCDE`).
Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
{jjdhr_heat_p}
encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
Input may omit the `‚Ç¢` prefix; output always includes it.

[[jjdt_silks]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
{jjdt_silks}::
A kebab-case display name.
Used for human-readable identification of
{jjdhr_heat_s}
and
{jjdpr_pace_s}.
Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`

== Records

=== {jjdgr_gallops}

[[jjdgr_gallops]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdgr_gallops}::
The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.

==== Members

* [[jjdgm_seed]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdgm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_firemark}
to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_nominate}.

=== {jjdhr_heat}

[[jjdhr_heat]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdhr_heat}::
A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
Contains zero or more
{jjdpr_pace_s}
representing discrete actions.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_firemark}.

==== Members

* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_firemark}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
primary key.
Type:
{jjdt_firemark}.
Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.

* [[jjdhm_silks]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_silks}
is the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.

* [[jjdhm_created]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_created}
records when the
{jjdhr_heat}
was nominated.
Format: YYMMDD.

* [[jjdhm_status]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_status}
indicates the
{jjdhr_heat_p}
lifecycle state.
Values:
{jjdhe_current}
or
{jjdhe_retired}.

* [[jjdhm_order]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated‚üß
{jjdhm_order}
lists
{jjdt_coronet_s}
in execution sequence.
May be reordered via
{jjdo_rail}.

* [[jjdhm_seed]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_seed}
is the next
{jjdt_coronet}
index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
Incremented by
{jjdo_slate}.

* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdhm_paddock}
is the path to the paddock file containing
{jjdhr_heat}
context and background.
Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
{jjdt_firemark}
(without `‚Ç£` prefix).
Created by
{jjdo_nominate}.

==== Status Values

[[jjdhe_current]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdhe_current}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is active and accepting work.

[[jjdhe_retired]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdhe_retired}::
The
{jjdhr_heat}
is complete and archived.

=== {jjdpr_pace}

[[jjdpr_pace]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdpr_pace}::
A discrete action within a
{jjdhr_heat}.
Contains one or more
{jjdkr_tack_s}
representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
Keyed in JSON by
{jjdt_coronet}.

==== Members

* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdpm_coronet}
is the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
globally unique identity.
Type:
{jjdt_coronet}.
Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.

* [[jjdpm_silks]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdpm_silks}
is the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
display name.
Type:
{jjdt_silks}.

=== {jjdkr_tack}

[[jjdkr_tack]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
{jjdkr_tack}::
A snapshot of
{jjdpr_pace}
state and plan at a point in time.
{jjdkr_tack_s}
form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
Position 0 is the most recent; older
{jjdkr_tack_s}
follow.
New
{jjdkr_tack_s}
are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
The current
{jjdpr_pace}
state is always
`tacks[0].state`.

==== Members

* [[jjdkm_refined]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdkm_refined}
records when this
{jjdkr_tack}
was added.
Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.

* [[jjdkm_state]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdkm_state}
indicates the
{jjdpr_pace_p}
progress at this snapshot.
Values:
{jjdpe_rough},
{jjdpe_primed},
{jjdpe_complete},
or
{jjdpe_abandoned}.

* [[jjdkm_text]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
{jjdkm_text}
contains the plan specification.
Non-empty string describing what the
{jjdpr_pace}
should accomplish.

* [[jjdkm_direction]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_conditional‚üß
{jjdkm_direction}
contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
Required when
{jjdkm_state}
is
{jjdpe_primed};
forbidden otherwise.
Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.

==== State Values

[[jjdpe_rough]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdpe_rough}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
needs clarification before work can proceed.
When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_rough}
pace, the model is guided to refine the specification rather than begin execution.

[[jjdpe_primed]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdpe_primed}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
is ready for autonomous execution.
The
{jjdkm_direction}
field contains explicit guidance for the executing agent.
When context is assembled for a
{jjdpe_primed}
pace, the model proceeds without human confirmation.

[[jjdpe_complete]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdpe_complete}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
has been successfully finished.

[[jjdpe_abandoned]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
{jjdpe_abandoned}::
The
{jjdpr_pace}
was stopped without completion.

== Serialization

[[jjdz_charset]]
{jjdz_charset}::
The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 ¬ß5):
`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.

[[jjdz_encoding]]
{jjdz_encoding}::
The algorithm for encoding
{jjdt_firemark}
and
{jjdt_coronet}
values.
+
*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
+
. Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters
. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `‚Ç£` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
. To decode `‚Ç£XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
+
*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
+
. Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters
. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `‚Ç¢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
. To decode `‚Ç¢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
+
*Input flexibility:*
+
CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
Output always includes the prefix (`‚Ç£` or `‚Ç¢`).

== Arguments

*Note:* {jjda_file} appears as the first argument on all operations.
It is annotated `// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß` and uses the default value when not explicitly provided.

[[jjda_file]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axa_cli_option‚üß
{jjda_file}::
Path to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
JSON file.
Long: `--file`.
Short: none.
Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.

[[jjda_silks]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axa_cli_option‚üß
{jjda_silks}::
Kebab-case display name for the target entity.
Long: `--silks`.
Short: `-s`.

[[jjda_state]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axa_cli_option‚üß
{jjda_state}::
Target state for the
{jjdkr_tack}.
Long: `--state`.
Short: none.
Values:
{jjdpe_rough},
{jjdpe_primed},
{jjdpe_complete},
{jjdpe_abandoned}.

[[jjda_direction]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axa_cli_option‚üß
{jjda_direction}::
Execution guidance for autonomous operation.
Long: `--direction`.
Short: `-d`.
Required when
{jjda_state}
is
{jjdpe_primed};
forbidden otherwise.

[[jjda_created]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axa_cli_option‚üß
{jjda_created}::
Creation date in YYMMDD format.
Long: `--created`.
Short: `-c`.

[[jjda_status]]

== CLI

[[jjdx_vvx]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_program‚üß
{jjdx_vvx}::
The Voce Viva executor.
Local reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.

[[jjdx_cli]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_command_group‚üß
{jjdx_cli}::
The Job Jockey command group within
{jjdx_vvx}.
Invoked as `vvx jjx`.
All operations are subcommands of
{jjdx_cli}.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.

== Operations

Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
{jjdx_cli} owns all
{jjdgr_gallops}
file access (read, transform, atomic write).
Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
{jjda_file}.

{jjdpr_pace}

{jjdhr_heat}

{jjdpm_silks}
(non-empty kebab-case),

{jjdkr_tack}

(YYMMDD-HHMM),

or

(non-empty string)

The `paces` array is ordered per

(positional) ‚Äî target
{jjdhr_heat}

{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:

----

  "heat_silks": "...",

  "paddock_content": "...",
  "pace_coronet": "‚Ç¢XXXXX",
  "pace_silks": "...",
  "pace_state": "rough|primed",
  "tack_text": "...",
  "tack_direction": "..."
}

Fields `pace_coronet` through `tack_direction` are absent if no actionable pace exists.
Field `tack_direction` is present only when `pace_state` is
{jjdpe_primed}.
Field `paddock_content` contains the full text of the paddock file.

. Read
{jjdgr_gallops}
from file

{jjdhr_heat}
. Output `paddock_file` from
{jjdhm_paddock}
. Read paddock file contents, output as `paddock_content`
. Walk
{jjdhm_order}
array in sequence
. For each
{jjdt_coronet},
check `paces[coronet].tacks[0].state`
. If state is
{jjdpe_rough}
or
{jjdpe_primed}:
.. Output `pace_coronet`,
`pace_silks`,
`pace_state`
.. Output `tack_text` from `tacks[0].text`
.. If state is
{jjdpe_primed},
output `tack_direction` from `tacks[0].direction`

[[jjdo_parade]]
// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
==== {jjdo_parade}

{jjdhr_heat}

{jjdpr_pace_s}

{jjds_stdout} JSON object with structure:

{jjdgr_gallops}

{jjdt_firemark}

{jjdpe_rough}
or

or
